reasoning: The Artemis Accords have maintained a consistent pace of new signatories, averaging about one per month. Recent momentum has been exceptionally strong, with signatories in December 2024 (Liechtenstein), January 2025 (Finland), February 2025 (Estonia), April 2025 (Bangladesh), and May 2025 (Norway), showing that the diplomatic push continues under the new administration. Historically, the month of June has seen at least one new signatory in each of the past four years (Brazil in 2021, France in 2022, India and Ecuador in 2023, Armenia in 2024). Furthermore, the 68th plenary session of UN COPUOS is scheduled for June 25 to July 4, 2025, providing a premier international forum for space diplomacy that often acts as a catalyst for such agreements. While it is possible the timing might slip into July or that no country happens to finalize their signing procedures in June, the base rate combined with the historical 4-year June streak and the impending COPUOS session makes a June 2025 signatory highly probable.
